tossups.question: In one of this filmmaker’s commentary tracks for the Criterion collection, this man answers a telephone call during a scene that depicts three men saying goodbye to a small rural village after a funeral. A song by Pierce the Veil is inspired by one of this man’s films; that song is The Divine Zero. One of this director’s characters, a prominent journalist, helps a teenage revolutionary write his manifesto, (*) which he obsesses over in the bath. Those characters are played by Frances McDormand and Timothee Chalamet, respectively. This director frequently collaborates with Jason Schwartzmann, Adrien Brody, and Owen Wilson. This director notably used real animal fur in making his films, as opposed to the industry standard synthetic material. Those materials were used in his stop motion films like Fantastic Mr. Fox and Isle of Dogs. For 10 points, name this director of The Darjeeling Limited and The Grand Budapest Hotel.

bonuses.leadin: This word comes after black in the English title of a movie about a ranch hand who joins a cult, which rallies against greedy landlords, after being swindled by his boss. For 10 points each:
parts: Name this word, the last in the English title of a Fernando Meirelles movie about an aspiring photographer in a Brazilian favela who goes by the name Rocket.
Black God, White Devil was heavily influenced by this American film genre, often criticized for its derogatory depiction of Native Americans in movies like Stagecoach and The Searchers.

Musician Seu Jorge, one of the actors in City of God, also recorded music and acted for this director’s movie The Life Aquatic. A film by this visually eccentric director features an author of western stories in the title quirky family.
